title: NoteDecryptor::try_decrypt_sapling takes txid: &[u8] but only validates len==32 at call site
description: scanner.rs NoteDecryptor trait: try_decrypt_sapling receives txid: &[u8] (a raw byte slice). The length check (txid.len() != 32 → return None) is inside SaplingIvkDecryptor::try_decrypt_sapling but is not part of the trait contract. A future NoteDecryptor implementor may omit the length check and panic on txid_bytes.copy_from_slice(txid) if txid is the wrong length. The scan_tx call site passes &tx.hash which is a Vec<u8> from the proto — its length is not verified before the call. Suggested rewrite: change the parameter type to txid: &[u8; 32] so the type system enforces the constraint, and let scan_tx do the length check and convert before calling try_decrypt_sapling. This eliminates the hidden invariant that every implementation must independently validate.
